results.title: AI Search: How to Adapt Your Content Strategy in 2026
results.content:
​AI search renders a lot of these assets ineffective. This is because search users now get most of their top-level information directly from AI-search responses.

### Invest in alternative TOFU channels

While the role of TOFU content may appear diminished in organic search, it is still a highly relevant element of your content strategy.

​Here are some alternative TOFU channels that marketers could invest in:

 ​Social media
 Communities
 Newsletters
 Video-based content on YouTube channels

​Some of these marketing channels may have their own algorithm and AI related challenges. However, these are consumption-focused channels and can help significantly with narrative-building. [...] There is another key difference AI is an enabler in these other channels. In other words, AI has made it difficult for marketers to build visibility in search. On the contrary, AI can help marketers build greater reach through these other channels.

Generative AI tools, for example, can help you create hundreds of image and video assets with just a few clicks. This means marketers can put together an entire month of content in minutes. It can also strengthen your TOFU strategy. So, allocating resources to develop these channels can be a smart move for your business.

Investing your resources in building these channels can be good for your business. [...] By identifying the needles that move AI search, and also diversifying your content distribution channels such that you are invested in other platforms like social media, email newsletters, and communities, it is possible to build stronger brand awareness and replace your existing pipeline with a stronger pipeline acquired from multiple sources.

This helps protect your marketing from future disruptions while also making sure that your business is built to take advantage of the new opportunities that AI presents with respect to customer acquisition.

results.title: AI for Business Growth: How to Scale Your Marketing | JSMM
results.content:
If you want to scale your marketing with AI for business growth, do not just look for more output. 

Look for a stronger system. The right system helps you attract the right traffic, improve conversion, see what is working and make better decisions as you grow.

That is what JSMM’s Growth Partner service is built to do. It helps you:

 Build one clear growth plan so your channels, offers and goals work together.
 Connect traffic and lead generation through ads, content, SEO and funnels.
 Improve conversion after the click with better follow-up, testing and lead flow.
 See performance more clearly with live visibility into leads, pipeline and payback.
 Scale with stronger leadership so decisions stay focused on revenue rather than scattered activity. [...] Instead of starting from scratch for every blog, email or campaign asset, you can use AI to turn one strong idea into multiple pieces that support the same growth goal. 

That helps businesses, agencies, and internal teams produce more output without sacrificing consistency. 

It also takes the most repetitive parts of content optimization off your plate. AI can generate titles, meta descriptions, header structures and first-draft outlines based on search trends and performance patterns.

The point is not just faster writing. It is a better resource allocation.

Use AI here to: [...] AI for business growth

Many brands think AI will solve a growth problem on its own. It will not. AI is a strong tool, but it still needs a clear plan behind it.

Here are some of the reasons why most brands struggle to scale with AI:

### Weak strategy

If your offer is unclear, your funnel leaks leads, or your messaging does not connect, AI will only help you move in the wrong direction. Speed does not help if the system is off.

### Disconnected channels

Paid media, SEO, content, email and social often run in silos. Each channel may be working hard, but they are not working together. That creates waste and makes scaling harder.

### Poor Measurement

results.title: Why Ultra-Specific, Bottom of Funnel Content Wins in AI Search
results.content:
The thought process behind this SEO strategy is that customers search around problems they’re trying to solve:

 “What’s the best way to measure conversions?” (JTBD keyword)
 “What are the best content marketing agencies?” (Category keyword)
 “What are the best alternatives to X?” (Competitor/Alternative keyword)

Showing up as a solution to those pain-point searches is far more likely to drive conversions than the top-of-funnel queries many content teams were targeting to grow traffic. Since then, we’ve published multiple case studies with data to back this up.

Now, in the world of AI search, we think this bottom-of-funnel strategy is paramount. [...] For example, you wouldn’t type in your company size, marketing budget, which channels you’ve already tried, or other relevant details. Yet we all have that context — and it matters when making a purchase decision. We just don’t type it into Google. Instead, we click in and out of results, scanning for signs that someone understands us and our problems, and might be a good fit.

But with AI search, people do give it their context. It’s a conversation, not a search. That’s the whole point.
